faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my New Springville home?
faqs.answer: Signs include piles of sawdust-like wood shavings near baseboards or wooden structures, rustling sounds in walls, and sighting large black ants (especially winged ones) inside your home. Carpenter ants are most active during spring and evening hours. Our technicians provide free inspections to confirm infestations and locate nests.

faqs.question: What does carpenter ant control cost in New Springville?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control services in New Springville typically range from $350 to $2,000, depending on the severity of infestation, colony locations, and home size. Minor treatments start around $350, while extensive infestations requiring multiple treatments and structural access may reach $2,000. We provide free quotes after thorough property inspections.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most homeowners notice significant reduction in carpenter ant activity within 7-10 days of initial treatment. Complete colony elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks as worker ants carry treatment back to nests. We often schedule follow-up visits to ensure total eradication and provide ongoing monitoring to prevent re-infestation.

faqs.question: Can carpenter ants cause serious damage to my New Springville home?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants hollow out wood to create nesting galleries, which can compromise structural integrity over time. Unlike termites, they don't eat wood but excavate it, causing similar damage. New Springville's older homes with wooden structures are particularly vulnerable. Early detection and professional treatment prevent costly repairs and protect your property value.
